blurb:
Roscoe is an eight-year-old boxer-shepherd mix with a heart as big as his smile. He's 54 pounds of pure sweetness and the perfect blend of fun and affection. He's a happy, social boy who adores being around people and making new friends. His easygoing personality makes him a joy to have around.

He's dog-friendly and enjoys having canine buddies to play with, though he'd be just as content soaking up love as the center of attention. With his medium-to-low energy level, he can enjoy a playful romp or a nice walk around the neighborhood, then settle right in for cuddle time on the couch. He's smart, already housebroken and crate trained, and walks well on a leash.

Roscoe would thrive in an adult-only home or with a family that has kids 12 and up, where he can feel secure and cherished. He's neutered, fully vaccinated, and microchipped, all ready to start his next chapter. Whether it's playtime, walk time, or snuggle time, he'll be your loyal companion every step of the way.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect family dog personality makes him highly adoptable
• Eight-year-old senior with easygoing nature appeals to mature families
• Well-trained and ready-to-go status removes adoption barriers

blurb:
Coconut Milk is a six-year-old cat with a mustache and a lot of opinions about how things should go. She's got sass, she's got attitude, and she's got very particular ideas about who she wants to spend time with. This is not a cat who's going to pretend to be something she's not.

She's more reserved, the kind of cat who likes to observe everything from her favorite perch before deciding if she's interested. She prefers calm company and doing things on her terms. If you're looking for a cat who's going to be in your face all the time, this isn't your girl. If you want a cat with serious personality who'll keep things interesting, she might be perfect.

Coconut Milk is housetrained and vaccinated. She's part of their Home for the Holidays special, so her adoption fee is half price through December 31st. If you're ready for a cat who brings a little chaos and a lot of character, she's waiting.

Email catinquiry@angelsrescue.org to inquire, or submit an adoption application at angelsrescue.org.
scoring_notes:
• Holiday discount pricing creates urgency for potential adopters
• Honest personality description appeals to readers seeking authentic pets
• Sassy attitude and distinctive mustache provide memorable newsletter hook

blurb:
Molly started as a feral cat at a local car shop who slowly earned her foster parent's trust. She disappeared for a few weeks, then showed back up with a big belly. They couldn't leave her to have kittens outside, so they took her in. She had four babies on the Fourth of July, and now she's ready for her own fresh start.

She's not your normal cat in the best way possible. She loves belly rubs and will pretty much let you do whatever you want with her, though she might give you a little back talk about it. She's fully litter box trained and has her rabies vaccine. She isn't the most playful, but she loves to be cuddled and gaze out windows in the sun.

Molly is good with adults and children, and she doesn't mind dogs. She'll tell you when she's hungry with a very quiet meow, nothing aggressive. After raising her babies and earning trust from humans, she's ready to be someone's pampered house cat.

Releashed Rescue is based in Roswell. Email releashedrescue@gmail.com to learn more about Molly and get the adoption process started.
scoring_notes:
• Feral-to-family transformation story showcases remarkable resilience
• Unique personality traits like belly rubs create memorable character
• Ready for fresh start after successful motherhood appeals to readers

blurb:
Annalise is a grey and white Alaskan Malamute who's probably around nine or ten years old. She was an owner surrender and out of time at a high kill shelter. Her previous owners likely used her up from overbreeding, but she's ready for the retirement she deserves now. She loves to play but is also perfectly content to sit on the couch and watch TV with you.

She's sweet once she gets comfortable, but she's fearful and barks at new people at first. That's understandable given what she's been through. She loves other calm male dogs but won't tolerate crazy ones, so she knows what she likes. She was treated for ectopic ureters and is doing great, but she'll need to be on estrogen and proin for life. It's manageable with daily medication.

Annalise would do best in a quiet home without children where she can decompress and just be a loved family dog. She's looking for someone who has room in their heart and home for a lovable girl who's earned some peace and comfort in her golden years.
scoring_notes:
• Overbreeding survivor deserving retirement creates powerful advocacy story
• Senior Malamute's second chance narrative drives reader compassion
• High-maintenance medical and behavioral needs require dedicated adopter

blurb:
Root Beer is thirteen years old and has decided that moving his food bowl around the house is his personal hobby. Call it interior design. Call it whatever you want. This 85-pound Lab-Weimaraner mix has opinions about where dinner should be served and he's not shy about rearranging things to his liking.

Don't let his age fool you. He's still got serious enthusiasm for car rides, leisurely walks, and human attention. He's housetrained, gets along great with kids and other dogs, and knows how to make the most of every moment. He wants to share all of them with you too. Whether he's trotting beside you on a trail or cozying up for head scratches, this distinguished gentleman has figured out what matters.

Root Beer is part of a holiday adoption special with half-price adoption fees through December 31st. He'd be happy as a solo companion or with his friend Gunnie who's also looking for a home. Submit an adoption application online at angelsrescue.org and email inquiry@angelsrescue.org to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Thirteen-year senior with quirky food bowl habit makes compelling human interest story
• Advanced age suggests lengthy shelter stay requiring immediate newsletter attention
• Holiday discount and option to adopt with friend Gunnie increases adoption appeal

blurb:
Snowberry figured out how to open lever door handles. This is either the best news or the worst news, depending on how you feel about a cat who can let herself into whatever room she wants. She's a seven-year-old long-haired tabby with a sunny personality and zero respect for closed doors.

She's extremely friendly and sociable, the kind of cat who wants to be involved in whatever you're doing. She loves her crunchy food, lots of pets, and regular brushing sessions. She's fine with other cats and dogs, but she'd also be perfectly happy as an only cat if that's what works better for you.

Snowberry is fully vaccinated and housetrained. She just needs someone who appreciates a cat with a little independence and a lot of personality. If you've got lever handles in your house, you've been warned. If you've got round knobs, you're probably safe.

Angels Among Us Pet Rescue requires an adoption application and inquiry through their website. Email catinquiry@angelsrescue.org to get started, or fill out the adoption form at angelsrescue.org.
scoring_notes:
• Viral-worthy story about a door-opening cat creates instant reader engagement
• Highly adoptable with friendly personality and compatibility with other pets
• Unique skill makes for memorable newsletter content that readers will share

blurb:
Kathy is fourteen years old, weighs exactly 22 pounds, and has mastered the art of being outgoing without being demanding. She's a Cocker Spaniel mix with a friendly wag and bright eyes who figured out that good things come to dogs who are calm and well-behaved. She's crate-trained, handles being alone without barking or chewing, and is making solid progress on her potty training.

She loves people of all ages and gets along well with small dogs. She's not a constant cuddle dog, but she brings warmth and happy energy without needing to be the center of attention all the time. She walks nicely on a leash and is perfect for short, leisurely strolls around the neighborhood.

Kathy dreams of a single-level home with no stairs, or just a few, where she can wander around comfortably and enjoy the simple pleasures of life by your side. She's got so much love left to give and just needs someone who understands that senior dogs can be the easiest, most grateful companions you'll ever find. Submit an adoption application online at angelsrescue.org to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Fourteen-year senior status indicates critical need for newsletter feature coverage
• Perfect apartment dog appeals to urban newsletter readership demographic
• Heartwarming senior dog story creates emotional urgency for adoption action

blurb:
Callaghan is your new movie buddy. He's a 14-year-old guy who's perfected the art of finding the comfiest spot in the house and claiming it for nap time. He's not quite a lap cat, but he'll plop down right beside you on the couch and stay there for the whole movie.

He's curious about everything, especially what you're eating, but in a mellow, senior cat way. He doesn't mind being picked up, loud noises, or even getting his twice-daily insulin shots. The diabetes sounds scarier than it is. Add the shots to his meal routine and it becomes just part of the day. He's got no problem with kids or other cats, but he's not a fan of dogs.

Callaghan would love to be an only child or have some feline siblings. He's the kind of cat who just wants to be part of the family, whether you're active or spend your evenings on the couch. His adoption fee is half price through December 31st as part of their holiday special.

Email catinquiry@angelsrescue.org or submit an adoption application at angelsrescue.org to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Senior age and medical needs suggest extended shelter stay requiring urgent attention
• Heartwarming movie buddy story creates emotional connection with readers
• Holiday pricing and manageable diabetes care make adoption more accessible

blurb:
Judy is eight months old and has been living her best life despite being born with a heart condition called ventricular septal defect. She doesn't know she has it, which is exactly how it should be. She just spends her days playing with toys, purring loudly, and figuring out the most adorable ways to drape herself across your lap for naps.

She's had two echocardiograms and is doing great. Her foster family says playtime is her love language, so if you've got feather wands or string toys, you're already her favorite person. When she's not busy having fun, she's curled up on a soft blanket or snoozing in a sunny window. She got along perfectly with her siblings before they were adopted and loved playing with the adult cats in a temporary foster home.

Judy would do best with gentle kids who understand how to play respectfully with a kitten, and she'd love another kitty friend in her new home. She's fully vetted and ready to go. The heart condition just means she'll need regular vet check-ups, but she's thriving and living life to the fullest.

Mostly Mutts is a rescue organization serving the Atlanta area. Contact them to learn more about Judy and get the adoption process started.
scoring_notes:
• Heart condition kitten living her best life creates emotional connection
• Young, playful personality appeals to families seeking active pet
• Medical needs may deter some adopters despite thriving condition

blurb:
Doli showed up eleven years into her life with sky blue eyes and the kind of thick golden coat that makes people stop and stare. She's a husky mix who figured out somewhere along the way that barking is optional and wiggle dances are mandatory when her people come home. At 50 pounds, she's got the energy for walks and car rides but the manners to live quietly in an apartment or townhome.

She loves her crate but doesn't need to be locked in it. She'll sleep there, on the floor, or in the master closet that she's decided is her personal den. She's housetrained, doesn't chew things up, and has adjusted well to foster life despite being shy with new people at first. The catch is she's not interested in sharing her space with other animals. She'll ignore them if she has to, but she'd really rather they just go away.

Doli needs a home where she can be the only pet. If you've got a quiet household and want a calm, well-behaved companion who still gets excited about the good stuff, she's going to fit right in. Submit an adoption application online and email inquiry@angelsrescue.org to get started.
scoring_notes:
• Eleven-year senior status indicates long shelter wait needing newsletter exposure
• Striking appearance and quiet apartment-friendly nature appeal to urban readers
• Only-pet requirement limits adopters but creates urgency for right match

blurb:
Charlotte has been waiting in foster care for nearly six years. She's a fantastic cat who loves people once she warms up, but she's been passed over again and again. Her foster mom has taken great care of her and helps manage her mild allergies, but Charlotte is wondering if that's why people don't want her.

She's shy when she first meets you, but once she trusts you, she turns into a big cuddle bug. She gets along well with other cats as long as they don't chase her around. The allergies aren't a big deal, just something to keep an eye on, and her foster mom can tell you exactly how to handle it.

Charlotte really wants one of those forever homes she keeps hearing about. After six years of waiting, she deserves someone who will see past the mild allergy management and recognize her for the sweet, loving cat she is. She's house trained and ready to go.

The Cats Meow is based in Atlanta. Email Info@catsmeowrescue.org to learn more about Charlotte and start the adoption process.
scoring_notes:
• Six years in foster care creates urgent newsletter-worthy story
• Heartbreaking narrative of being repeatedly passed over drives reader empathy
• Sweet personality with manageable needs appeals to patient adopters

blurb:
Evie is an eight-year-old white pit bull mix who was born deaf, but you'd never guess it from watching her. She's smart, attentive, and responds quickly to everything around her. She just does it all through sight instead of sound. She's the kind of dog who forms strong bonds fast and wants to be wherever you are.

She's good with kids and enjoys playing with other dogs after slow introductions, but she'd be perfectly happy being the only pup soaking up all the love. Toys and treats are her favorites, and her food motivation makes her eager to learn. She doesn't need a backyard to be happy, though she'd enjoy one. What matters most to her are family playtime and plenty of cuddles.

Evie's already spayed, fully vaccinated, microchipped, housebroken, and crate trained. She's ready to settle right into her forever home. Email Barkville or submit an application online to meet this beautiful girl who's just waiting for a family to love her as much as she'll love them.
scoring_notes:
• Deaf pit bull defying stereotypes creates inspiring feature story
• Eight-year-old senior needs showcase urgency for older pet adoption
• Strong adaptive skills demonstrate resilience that resonates with readers
